thread.title: Delhi Meitei Body Condemns Chidambaram for Advocating Regional Autonomy in Manipur | Guwahati News - Times of India
thread.title_full: Delhi Meitei Body Condemns Chidambaram for Advocating Regional Autonomy in Manipur | Guwahati News - Times of India

author: TNN
title: Delhi Meitei Body Condemns Chidambaram for Advocating Regional Autonomy in Manipur | Guwahati News - Times of India
text: Guwahati: The Delhi Meitei Coordinating Committee has condemned former Union home minister P Chidambaram’s recent post on X, which he later deleted following pressure from his own party colleagues.“While DMCC agrees with his first point, which notes that deploying 5,000 security personnel will not resolve the conflict,” his third point where he stated, “It is more understandable that the Meiteis, Kuki-Zos and the Nagas can live together in one state only if they have genuine regional autonomy” is “deeply concerning,” the committee’s media coordinator stated in a statement.“This indicates that peaceful coexistence under the current governance framework is not feasible. Conversely, he proposes/suggests that if separate systems are established for the Kuki, Naga and Meitei communities, it could pave the way for lasting peace in Manipur. Such a statement is highly insensitive, especially during these tumultuous times in Manipur,” the coordinator stated.“Our primary concern is ensuring balanced development for all of Manipur. However, we will oppose any policy that fosters division or promotes divisive agendas based on ethnicity or religion,” he added. The committee also appealed to all elected Congress MLAs in Manipur to take responsibility for sensitising the party’s central leadership to the ongoing conflict. TNNWe also published the following articles recentlyDelhi Meitei committee condemns Chidambarams post batting for regional autonomy in ManipurThe Delhi Meitei Coordinating Committee (DMCC) criticizes former Union Home Minister P Chidambaram's deleted X post regarding the Manipur conflict. While agreeing that increased security forces won't bring peace, the DMCC condemns Chidambaram's suggestion of separate systems for different communities, calling it insensitive and divisive.115568461Both Meitei & Kuki groups bin move by Manipur NDA MLAsTensions escalate in Manipur as Meitei and Kuki groups reject a resolution passed by NDA legislators aimed at quelling violence. The Meitei community demands a crackdown on Kuki militants after the killing of six people in a Jiribam shelter, while the Kuki-Zo community condemns the resolution as ethnic persecution and calls for government intervention.115462376Meitei, Kuki bodies reject peace resolutionsBoth Meitei and Kuki-Zo groups in Manipur have rejected resolutions passed by the ruling BJP and its allies aimed at restoring peace. The resolutions called for action against Kuki militants after the killing of six people in Jiribam.115462221
